summary_draft: China-based exchange launched in 2013 that later became inactive; possible overseas-lineage relation to AEX needs explicit review before any dead-side promotion.

non_archive.title: Cryptowisser BTC38 review with inactive/dead note
BTC38 / 比特时代 profile stating May 2013 launch and later stop
non_archive.purpose: launch month/year and later AEX-lineage claim

evidence_notes: Current terminal evidence is tracker-driven and should not be over-read.
AEX-lineage claim must be treated as provisional until better source quality is secured.
Need archive plus at least two non-archive sources before public_ready promotion.

event_candidates.title: BTC38 launch window
event_candidates.description: Later exchange profiles describe BTC38 / 比特时代 as beginning operation in May 2013.

event_candidates.title: Site flagged inaccessible / inactive by trackers
event_candidates.description: By mid-2019, third-party exchange trackers said the site could not be accessed and marked the venue inactive or dead.

lineage_notes: Possible relation to AEX / Bit.cc is not yet clean enough for predecessor/successor modeling.
Do not promote to dead or rebranded until archive and stronger non-tracker material are found.
risks: AEX relation may reflect lineage, migration, or only later community labeling
current status may be dead-side in practice but evidence is too soft for hard classification
final_notes: Keep BTC38 conservative as inactive and lineage-sensitive for now.
Next step: find stronger archived official material around final shutdown or migration.
